chunk_summary: This section outlines decision nodes for outpatient follow-up of moderate acute malnutrition (MAM), emphasizing timely escalation based on anthropometry, clinical signs, and adherence audits at Day 0, Day 7/14, Week 4, and Week 8.
learning_objectives: Understand the decision-making process for outpatient follow-up of MAM at specific time points.
Identify high-risk children using WHO prioritization factors and schedule appropriate follow-ups.
Learn to assess adherence, anthropometry, and clinical signs to guide escalation or continuation of care.
Recognize failure thresholds and initiate investigations or referrals for non-responding cases.
key_facts: Day 0: Confirm MUAC (115–119 mm) and/or WHZ/WLZ, check for oedema, appetite, and danger signs, and classify high-risk cases for 7-day follow-up.
Day 7/14: Repeat anthropometry, verify adherence, and escalate care if weight loss, falling MUAC, or clinical deterioration is observed.
Week 4: If no improvement despite adherence, initiate illness screening (e.g., TB, anemia, UTI) and consider referral for comorbidities or safeguarding concerns.
Week 8: Failure threshold for non-responding cases; initiate staged investigations and escalate care as needed.
High-risk factors include MUAC 115–119 mm, WAZ < -3, age < 24 months, prior SAM, comorbidities, or severe social adversity.
reference_quote: Classify as high-risk when any WHO prioritization factor is present (MUAC 115–119 mm, WAZ < -3, age under 24 months, relapse, prior SAM, comorbidity or disability, or severe social adversity) and schedule review in 7 days.
